canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, WikiDatarecent is consistently presented as a measurement instrument for evaluating knowledge editing in large language models. The dataset is defined as focusing on "recent, updated facts from Wikidata." It is explicitly used to measure the behavior of `Knowledge editing`, with some papers specifying its application for tasks of "knowledge insertion and knowledge modification." In addition to its primary use, some work also employs WikiDatarecent to assess the constructs of `Locality` and `Linguistic quality` following an edit. In experimental contexts, it is often used alongside other datasets like ZsRE and WikiDatacounterfact.
papers: Can Knowledge Editing Really Correct Hallucinations?
In-Context Editing: Learning Knowledge from Self-Induced Distributions
Precise Localization of Memories: A Fine-grained Neuron-level Knowledge Editing Technique for LLMs
Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning

relationships.evidence: "We select three datasets including knowledge insertion and knowledge modification in our experiments: WikiDatacounterfact (Cohen et al., 2024), WikiDatarecent (Cohen et al., 2024) and ZsRE (Levy et al., 2017)."

iclr25: Unlocking Efficient, Scalable, and Continual Knowledge Editing with Basis-Level Representation Fine-Tuning
